text: Handling deadlines effectively is an essential skill in both work and study. Good time management helps you break large projects into smaller tasks, making them less overwhelming. Prioritization is also key—decide which tasks are most urgent and important, and focus on those first. Sometimes, you may need to practice scope management, which means saying no to extra requests that would overload your schedule. Finally, never underestimate the power of communication. If you realize you cannot meet a deadline, inform your team or teacher early. This allows everyone to adjust plans, reducing stress and maintaining trust.
